name: Analytics Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof that the webanalytics CustomTracker contract+injector swap mechanic (#1012) is conformant. The runtime (the CustomTrackerRegistry swap registry, the native-first NoopTracker default, and the reference vendor adapters) is relocated to Frontier UI (#1918, WE holds zero executable); its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788/#1790) driving the WE-owned analytics vector suite (#1918: predicate matchers over the recorded-call log — routing / swap-reroute / arg-order / absence / count, #1816/#1847) against the FUI binding. The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside a cross-origin iframe.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof that the webanalytics CustomTracker contract+injector swap mechanic (#1012) is conformant. The runtime — the CustomTrackerRegistry swap registry, the native-first NoopTracker default, and the reference vendor adapters — is relocated to Frontier UI (#1918; WE holds zero executable, #1282). Its conformance runs through the plateau-hosted conformance runner (#1788 / #1790), which drives the WE analytics vector suite (#1918: predicate matchers over the recorded-call log — routing / swap-reroute / arg-order / absence / count, #1816/#1847) against the FUI binding.

Verified contract

WE owns only the build-agnostic vectors and the type-only CustomTracker contract; the registry + reference adapter impl is FUI's, the runner is plateau's. The predicate suite asserts the invariants: identify/track/page/group route to the resolved backend, redefining the default reroutes subsequent calls, an explicit per-call key spares siblings, page(category, name) honors the Segment arg order, and calls accumulate on the recorded log. The pass/fail panel is rendered at plateau-origin inside an iframe — the cross-origin boundary (#1788 ratified (b)) that reaches the shared, neutral runner with no implementer→runner edge. A green result proves conformance independent of how the runtime is built (#899).
